definitions: The theory or practice of shielding a country's domestic industries from foreign competition by taxing imports.
A policy of protecting domestic industries against foreign competition by means of tariffs, subsidies, import quotas, or other restrictions or handicaps placed on the imports of foreign competitors.
examples: The government implemented protectionism to save local jobs.
Critics argued that protectionism would lead to higher prices.
Many developing nations have historically used protectionism.
